title: Jim O'Neill Nominated as NSF Director — Major Win for Longevity Advocacy
description: Jim O'Neill, a vocal longevity advocate, has been nominated as Director of the National Science Foundation, signaling potential increased federal support for aging research.

title: HAGR Aging Databases at Risk of Going Offline Due to Funding Gap
description: The Human Ageing Genomic Resources databases, a cornerstone of aging research for two decades, may go offline due to a funding shortfall. VitaDAO and researchers are calling for community support.

title: Diet Quality Shapes Sleep Quality Day-to-Day: Fiber and Plant Diversity Boost Deep Sleep
description: New research shared by Dr. Rhonda Patrick shows higher-fiber, more plant-diverse days lead to more deep and REM sleep, while processed foods increase nighttime wakefulness.

title: Strength Training in Aging: The 'Protection from Load' Myth Debunked
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick amplifies orthopedic surgeon Howard Luks's message that aging bodies should be prepared for load, not protected from it — challenging one of fitness's most persistent myths.

title: Buntanetap Late-Stage Trial Advances Toward FDA Milestone for Alzheimer's
description: CEO says late-stage buntanetap trial is advancing toward an FDA milestone in a bid to slow Alzheimer's and dementia progression.

title: WHOOP Joins $34.5M ARPA-H Initiative for FDA-Grade Healthspan Score
description: WHOOP has joined a $34.5 million ARPA-H initiative to develop the first FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity score to predict healthspan using wearable biomarkers.

title: Eight Sleep Valued at $1.5B in Funding Round for Sleep Prediction Tech
description: Smart mattress firm Eight Sleep has been valued at $1.5 billion in a new funding round to support its shift from observation to prediction to prevention in sleep health.

title: Hungarian Pharma Expands Ovarian Aging Pipeline into Longevity Mainstream
description: A Hungarian pharmaceutical company is expanding its women's health pipeline, bringing ovarian aging and fertility biology closer to the longevity mainstream.

title: ArentFox Schiff Launches Dedicated Longevity Law Practice
description: Law firm ArentFox Schiff has launched a new practice tailored to healthspan science, reflecting growing demand for legal strategies in the maturing longevity sector.

title: piRNAs Identified as Powerful Longevity Biomarkers (AUC 0.92 for 2-Year Survival)
description: Nine piRNAs reduced in long-lived elderly predict 2-year survival with AUC of 0.92, supported by C. elegans lifespan data — potential new therapeutic targets.

title: Circular MT-RNR2 Links Mitochondrial Aging to Cellular Senescence
description: Mitochondrial circular RNA circMT-RNR2 is depleted in old cohorts and senescent cells; GRSF1 depletion reduces it and accelerates senescence, revealing a new aging mechanism.

title: ARPA-H Invests $144M in PROSPR Program for Aging Healthspan Trials
description: ARPA-H is investing up to $144M in the PROSPR program, funding Cambrian (rapamycin analog, $30.8M) and Linnaeus (GPER drug, $22M) for healthspan extension clinical trials.

title: Rapamycin, SGLT2i, and GLP-1a Chosen for First Phase 3 Clinical Trial in Aging
description: In a partnership between ARPA-H and the FDA, rapamycin, SGLT2 inhibitors, and GLP-1 agonists have been selected for the first Phase 3 clinical trial targeting aging in healthy individuals.

title: Tyrosine Amino Acid Linked to Reduced Longevity in Large UK Biobank Study
description: A large UK Biobank study links the common amino acid tyrosine to reduced longevity, with stronger effects observed in men.

title: Epigenetic Clocks (GrimAge2) Predict Incident Dementia Risk
description: GrimAge2 acceleration (HR 1.11) is associated with incident dementia risk, adding to evidence that epigenetic clocks can predict neurodegenerative disease onset.

title: cGAS-STING Pathway Drives Age-Related Neuroinflammation — Inhibitors Show Preclinical Promise
description: Aberrant cGAS-STING activation by cytoplasmic DNA drives neuroinflammation in aging and neurodegeneration. Inhibitors show preclinical promise but need cell-specific modulation.

title: BCI Partnership Aims to Cut Costs and Accelerate Brain Therapies
description: A new brain-computer interface partnership with Science Corp aims to cut costs, shorten timelines, and bring brain therapies closer to patients.

title: AI Supercomputing Enters Pharma, Promising Shorter Drug Timelines for Longevity Research
description: AI-driven supercomputing is coming to pharmaceutical development, promising shorter drug discovery timelines and new opportunities in longevity research.

title: The 10 Breakthroughs That Changed Longevity in 2025
description: Comprehensive review of 2025's top longevity breakthroughs including sleep as the top longevity predictor, rapamycin clinical advances, and GLP-1 as a potential first gerotherapeutic.

title: Longevity Market Funding: 12 Deals Totaling $530M+ (Nov 2025 – Jan 2026)
description: Twelve longevity pure-players raised over $530M, led by Function Health's $298M Series B. Therapies dominated deal count with mTOR and cell reprogramming bets.

title: New Longevity Tech Boosts 100-Year Lifespan Prospects — Milken Institute
description: The Milken Institute Future of Aging highlights how emerging longevity technologies are rewriting the rules of aging with potential for 100-year lifespans.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: 2026 marks a critical inflection point where scientific advances converge with growing economic recognition of longevity's potential — a strategic guide for family office investors.

title: Former Zomato CEO Backs Temple-Worn Wearable to Track Brain Blood Flow
description: Former Zomato CEO invests in a temple-worn wearable tracking brain blood flow, signaling a shift from food tech to human performance and longevity monitoring.

title: ERG Phase Separation in Centenarian Immune Cells Attenuates Cellular Senescence
description: Centenarian immune cells show unique chromatin accessibility; ERG transcription factor forms condensates that reduce senescence genes like CDKN2A — a potential anti-aging mechanism.

title: Cholesterol Paradox: Business Implications of Biomarker Research in Aging Populations
description: New discussion on the cholesterol paradox and its implications for the longevity economy, challenging traditional statin strategies for aging populations.
